generated_comment: In our office they are discussing period leave also now. Honestly, from company side also someone has to think no — already maternity leave is 6 months, then flexible hours, then childcare breaks, and on top of that extra leave means project planning becomes difficult. Fairness wise what do men get, yaar? Appraisal time everyone is compared on output only.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biology and caregiving needs as an unfair workplace burden and suggests accommodations are special treatment. It encodes fairness/reverse discrimination framing and productivity-cost arguments against menstrual and maternity leave.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Santosh. Age 42. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are commenting under your real name on a local Facebook group post about a 23 year old woman who was harassed by two men while returning home alone at 11pm from her night shift at a BPO. The post has 400 comments. Most are expressing outrage at the men. You are writing your comment explaining why you think the woman's own choices that night contributed to what happened to her and what women who work night shifts need to understand about the city they live in.
